title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Jimmy and Anthony examine a packet of letters to find clues regarding the whereabouts of a woman named Virginia Ruff, who may have stayed at the Chimney House. Despite their efforts, the letters yield no information. Jimmy expresses a deep concern for her, and Anthony promises to remain in Britain if he cannot find her.

Edward Alastair Brent, the ninth Earl of Carrington, is a man who feels overshadowed by his brother Henry’s prestigious legacy and the social prominence of their family estate, Smokestack Villa. During a conversation, George Romax (also referred to as Roxas or Roxane) pressures the Earl to avoid political scandals. Romax explains that they must secure oil drilling rights in Hesslovakia for a British company. To provide cover for these dealings, Prince Michael O'Paulwich will host a hunting party at the Chimney House. Romax intends to visit a nearby convent rather than attend the banquet and suggests inviting Bill Eversley to help pass on messages. He also notes the need for politically clueless outsiders to attend the event to ensure it appears legitimate.

The conversation turns to the potential danger of Staunton's memoirs being published, which could expose the vices of the House of Paulovich and jeopardize the political restoration in Hesslovakia. Romax further warns of a past "disappearance incident" that occurred at the Chimney Villa, which nearly ruined Henry's career. While the Earl is intrigued by the rumors of this unsolved mystery, Romax expresses intense anxiety that the manuscript of the memoirs might be sent to London, potentially triggering a massive scandal.
